meaning: adj.朝海的,海那一边的adv.向海,向海那一边
examples: According to Davis' scheme, the shoreline is being extended in a seaward direction because of the "land-building" role of mangroves, which, by trapping sediments over time, extend the shore.
The houses on the seaward side of the road were all in ruins.
A barge was about a hundred yards away, waiting to return seaward.
The Grade II listed building closed in 2011 and was replaced by a new community hospital in Seaward Way.
Ebb-tide delta: an accretionary deposit of sand found on the seaward side of an inlet and usually formed by tidal currents.
